name: env-switcher
description: Quickly switch between .env files by symlinking from a .envs folder

commands.title: Env Switcher: Open .envs Folder in System File Manager
Env Switcher: Backup .envs to Local Storage
Env Switcher: Restore .envs from Local Backup
Env Switcher: Save Vault Snapshot Now
Env Switcher: Open Vault Folder for This Workspace
Env Switcher: Link a Profile to a Folder…
Env Switcher: Remove .env Symlink from a Folder…

envSwitcher.envFolder.description: Folder name containing your .env files (relative to workspace root)

envSwitcher.targetFile.description: Target .env filename to symlink to (relative to each target directory)

envSwitcher.targetDirectories.description: Directories (relative to workspace root) where the .env symlink is created. Use "." for the workspace root. Example: [".", "frontend", "backend"].

envSwitcher.autoBackup.description: Automatically back up the .envs folder to VS Code's local storage whenever it changes. Backups survive repo deletion and can be restored into a fresh clone.

envSwitcher.backupDebounceMs.description: Debounce delay in milliseconds before refreshing views and running an automatic backup after .envs or .env files change. Set to 0 to disable debouncing.

envSwitcher.excludedFolders.description: Folder names ignored when scanning the workspace (kept for compatibility; target dirs are configured with envSwitcher.targetDirectories).

envSwitcher.vaultEnabled.description: Keep a local versioned vault of your .envs tree (content-addressed snapshots with history). Stored outside the project; disable if you do not want plaintext copies on disk.

envSwitcher.vaultAutoSnapshot.description: When the vault is enabled, automatically append a snapshot after .envs changes (same debounce as backup) and after symlink operations.

enumDescriptions: Inside the editor's global storage (user-specific, with the extension).
Under ~/.env-switcher-vault on this machine (chmod 700 on macOS/Linux when possible).
envSwitcher.vaultLocation.description: Where the env vault root directory is created.

envSwitcher.vaultMaxVersions.description: Maximum number of vault snapshots to retain per workspace (oldest deleted after each new snapshot).

envSwitcher.vaultSkipUnchanged.description: Skip auto-snapshots when file contents match the latest vault snapshot (same SHA-256 fingerprint).

view/title.when: view == envSwitcher.guide || view == envSwitcher.envFiles || view == envSwitcher.projectFolders

viewsWelcome.contents:
Open a **folder** to use Env Switcher.

You will need a `.envs` directory at the project root (or accept a restore prompt if a backup exists).

[Open Env Switcher settings](command:envSwitcher.openEnvSwitcherSettings)

No `.envs` folder here yet.

**What this is:** real env files live under `.envs/`. Each **target directory** (see settings) has a **symlink** `.env` → one of those files.

**Setup:** create `.envs/` at the workspace root and add files (e.g. `dev.env`, `prod.env`).

[Switch which profile is linked](command:envSwitcher.switchEnv)

Use **Guide & actions** (first view in this sidebar) for click-through flows, vault history, and backups.

[Open Env Switcher settings](command:envSwitcher.openEnvSwitcherSettings)

**Target directories** are folders that get a `.env` symlink (see `envSwitcher.targetDirectories`).

Right-click a row to **Assign** or **Remove** a link, or use **Guide & actions** → Symlinks for the same steps without the tree.

[Switch active profile](command:envSwitcher.switchEnv)

[Open settings](command:envSwitcher.openEnvSwitcherSettings)

scripts.test: npm run test:unit && npm run test:smoke && npm run test:vscode
